title: Mr Spritz Goes To Washington

jokes.text: Three Stooges parody with aging health issues - 'He don't have health insurance' and 'that was my paralyzed side'
jokes.punchline: Modern reality of aging performers without healthcare
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of nostalgia - takes beloved slapstick and adds dark reality of aging entertainers

jokes.text: Curly Four? I need a quarter for the bus
jokes.setup: Reference to multiple Curlys in Three Stooges history
jokes.punchline: Pathetic state of needing bus fare
jokes.explanation: Good economy and specific reference that fans appreciate

jokes.text: Not the swear jar. It's the only thing holding back the filth
jokes.setup: House shaking from jets threatens swear jar
jokes.punchline: Swear jar as last defense against moral decay
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's twisted logic about morality

jokes.text: Nutty fudgkins
jokes.setup: Homer needs to curse but has swear jar
jokes.explanation: Simple character humor - Homer's childish swear substitute

jokes.text: DO YOU HEAR A NOISE? / WHAT?
jokes.punchline: Characters shouting to communicate over obvious noise
jokes.explanation: Standard visual comedy setup but effective execution

jokes.text: If I stop pedaling, I'll die. But it still beats U.S. Air
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline structure with relatable airline complaint

jokes.text: Why do we gotta watch The Divine Secrets of the Ya-Ya Sisterhood? The boss. He's got a thing for the ya-yas
jokes.setup: Questioning movie choice on private jet
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke, somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Ew! I got Bart's veal. I got Lisa's plankton kebobs. Ew! Marge germs
jokes.punchline: Each family member's food reflects their character
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Bart eating veal, Lisa eating plankton shows their personalities

jokes.text: This is not a Delta hub
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer deadpan non-sequitur in chaotic situation

jokes.text: It was an application to open a Cinnabon stand
jokes.explanation: Good bureaucratic absurdity with specific brand reference

jokes.text: Vote Quimby. Vote Quimby. Vote Quimby. Ahh! Without those noisy planes, I can finally hear my kitten purr
jokes.setup: Mayor campaigning while planes are loud
jokes.punchline: Reveals he moved the planes for his personal kitten
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - corrupt politician's absurdly selfish motivation

jokes.text: That's your voice? Now, I regret building you that opera house
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fat Tony line - implies past corruption while being comedically petty

jokes.text: We make the laws. We print the money. And we breed the super-soldiers
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of secret super-soldier program
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from mundane to absurd conspiracy theory

jokes.text: The dog has eaten all its hair
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - single line conveys extreme situation perfectly

jokes.text: Stay plugged in. I will find you
jokes.setup: Addressing electrical outlets while house shakes
jokes.explanation: Good Homer absurdity - treating appliances as sentient beings

jokes.text: Cookie's number one on the West Side. But this is the East Side. Who is this lady?
jokes.punchline: Wrong side of town, questioned competence
jokes.explanation: Solid setup-punchline exposing incompetent real estate agent

jokes.text: I'll give you three crayons and my milk
jokes.explanation: Cute child logic applied to adult transaction

jokes.text: I'm walking away
jokes.explanation: Good callback extending the child negotiator bit

jokes.text: The shaking's so bad, all my omelets have become frittatas
jokes.explanation: Excellent Marge line - specific cooking knowledge applied to absurd situation

jokes.text: Also I taste copper
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - specific medical detail makes death more absurd

jokes.text: I see a bad moon rising - That's CCR
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay confusion between CPR and CCR

jokes.text: I guess there's only one way out of our problems: A murder & suicide pact
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer dark humor - suggests extreme solution casually

jokes.text: Jesse 'The Body' Ventura, Sonny Bono, Gopher from Love Boat
jokes.punchline: Escalates from real to increasingly minor celebrities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from legitimate to absurd celebrity politicians

jokes.text: Have your mom mail back the hat after, uh, you know...
jokes.punchline: Casually assumes child will die, wants hat back
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty character moment - callous celebrity behavior

jokes.text: Yeah, that's the spirit. Keep fighting. Where's my chai latte?
jokes.explanation: Good Krusty characterization - fake sincerity immediately abandoned

jokes.text: Immigration? Oh, uh, Teeny here is from Brazil. His uncle was the head monkey at the Bureau of Tourism
jokes.explanation: Absurd explanation mixing reality with cartoon logic

jokes.text: It's doing 13 while she's elevening your five
jokes.explanation: Creative way to suggest something inappropriate using numbers

jokes.text: Sure, no 12 off my ass
jokes.explanation: Good callback using the established numbering system

jokes.text: The Mississippi River is now the Mississippi Reagan. And my good friend Frankenstein is now Frankenreagan. Bleh!
jokes.explanation: Good political satire escalating to absurdity with Frankenstein

jokes.text: Bob Dole likes to hear Bob Dole talk about Bob Dole. Bob Dole
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on his own speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Good caricature of real political figure's speech pattern

jokes.text: Russian hooker. You tell me
jokes.explanation: Efficient dark humor revealing Krusty's seedy past

jokes.text: I did find out one fact: she was a guy
jokes.explanation: Escalates the scandal with additional twist

jokes.text: You said, 'You're going to vomit on their mother's graves'
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - innocent attempt becomes offensive disaster

jokes.text: She had more acting talent in one boob than most women have in their entire rack
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate sexist humor showing Krusty's tone-deafness

jokes.text: Yeah, listen to the tomato with the melons
jokes.explanation: Continues the sexist food metaphor pattern, predictable

jokes.text: For a thousand, I can have somebody whacked. Uh, it's a joke. When you give me that look, it's a joke
jokes.punchline: Backtracking on murder offer based on reaction
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with character awareness of audience reaction

jokes.text: How can I prove we're live? Penis
jokes.setup: News anchor proving broadcast is live
jokes.punchline: Uses word that would be censored if pre-recorded
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about live vs. recorded broadcast standards

jokes.text: Take a look at this incredibly offensive sketch from his show
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for the UN sketch to follow

jokes.text: Oh, my cheese, she stinks. No, wait. it's me
jokes.explanation: Basic stereotype humor with mild subversion

jokes.text: Hey, man. I call for a joint session. This vote is dreadlocked
jokes.punchline: Marijuana and dreadlock puns on parliamentary terms
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay mixing political and cultural references

jokes.text: Hello there, sailor. I'm here and fabulous. Now, I'd like to make a motion. Woo!
jokes.explanation: Predictable stereotype humor with obvious double entendre

jokes.text: You can't judge me by that sketch. It was a different time, 1998
jokes.punchline: 1998 was not that long ago for attitudes to change
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of celebrity non-apologies claiming 'different times'

jokes.text: I'm Carlton Witherspoon III. Where's my five iron?
jokes.setup: Krusty trying to change identity from Jewish to WASP
jokes.punchline: Adopts stereotypical rich white name and golf obsession
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's desperation and poor judgment

jokes.text: Krusty, why did you just drop your pants? I needed a joke to go out on
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's purely for comedy structure
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about comedy structure while staying in character

jokes.text: He's really looking out for me, the average Joe Sixtooth. Where'd you get yourself another tooth? Sidewalk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus character moment - disturbing poverty humor

jokes.text: A voice that will go: Ahooga! Wah-wah! Wac ka-wac ka-wac ka!
jokes.setup: Promising to be voice for people
jokes.punchline: Voice is literally cartoon sound effects
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - Krusty can't escape his clown identity

jokes.text: The pipes under my toilet don't lead nowhere
jokes.explanation: Good Springfield citizen absurdity with nonsensical problem

jokes.text: And watch out for the 612
jokes.setup: Krusty warning crowd as he leaves
jokes.explanation: Good setup-payoff with visual comedy timing

jokes.text: Welcome to Fox News. Your voice for evil
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire with perfect economy and quotability

jokes.text: I have a name. Yes, I'm sure you do, comrade
jokes.explanation: Good media bias satire with dismissive treatment

jokes.text: He hasn't won yet. You make a very adulterous point.
jokes.setup: Candidate corrects he hasn't won yet
jokes.punchline: Anchor uses 'adulterous' instead of 'astute' - Freudian slip suggesting affairs
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay suggesting unconscious bias and scandal obsession

jokes.text: You make a very adulterous point
jokes.explanation: Simple malapropism humor showing host's incompetence

jokes.text: Photographs have been modified to include Krusty
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of political advertising dishonesty

jokes.text: I'm on pins and needles. And I'm on Pepto and Xanax.
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Marge's normal expression vs Homer's medicated version

jokes.text: I'm so nervous, I kissed my dog and gave my girlfriend a flea bath
jokes.punchline: Mixed up normal activities with pet activities
jokes.explanation: Good Homer confusion humor mixing categories

jokes.text: I won. I'm a senator. Congressman. Whatever
jokes.punchline: Doesn't know what office he won
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty character moment showing his ignorance

jokes.text: So relax, gun nuts. I can't touch you
jokes.explanation: Good political humor about Constitutional protections

jokes.text: So relax, gun nuts. I can't touch you.
jokes.punchline: Immediately reassures gun owners he's powerless
jokes.explanation: Great political satire - oath of office immediately undermined by special interest pandering

jokes.text: Freshmen congressmen don't introduce squat. You gotta pee in a bucket until your second term
jokes.explanation: Good political satire with absurd hazing imagery

jokes.text: Grover Cleveland sucks what? Leave that, lest we forget
jokes.punchline: Treating crude graffiti as important historical record
jokes.explanation: Clever historical humor about preserving inappropriate graffiti

jokes.text: I say we make them out of chocolate. Kids eat them. Why fight it?
jokes.explanation: Good Krusty logic - childish but oddly practical solution

jokes.text: We congressmen are full of it. I blather on for hours. Yap-yap-yap-yap-yap-yap!
jokes.punchline: Self-aware politician admitting to empty talk
jokes.explanation: Good political satire with self-aware politician

jokes.text: Did I promise to be a slave to big oil? No. Well, then none
jokes.punchline: Only kept promises he didn't make
jokes.explanation: Excellent political satire about broken campaign promises

jokes.text: How could you vote? You're only 10. This is not about me. Or how many times I voted
jokes.punchline: Implies she voted multiple times illegally
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment showing her political engagement

jokes.text: That's jelly. From the unknown toaster
jokes.setup: Homer claiming document stained with patriot blood
jokes.punchline: It's actually jelly from unknown toaster
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment with Tomb of Unknown Soldier parody

jokes.text: No one usually shows up unless there's a vote. Well, then why are you here? I steal stuff when everyone's gone
jokes.explanation: Good corruption humor with honest thief congressman

jokes.text: Beat it, Waxie. No. Let's hear him out. He looks a little like Walter Mondale
jokes.punchline: Trusting him because he resembles failed politician
jokes.explanation: Odd political reference humor about trusting based on appearance

jokes.text: Wrong. That's wrong
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining how bill becomes law
jokes.explanation: Sets up cynical view of how government really works

jokes.text: Sir, I studied under Ed McMahon
jokes.setup: Asked if he can drink congressman under table
jokes.punchline: References Tonight Show sidekick as drinking mentor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer line with specific cultural reference

jokes.text: You remind me of my high school drinking coach
jokes.explanation: Good Southern stereotype humor about drinking culture

jokes.text: You call this a bicameral legislature?
jokes.punchline: Uses sophisticated political term while causing chaos
jokes.explanation: Great Homer moment using big words inappropriately

jokes.text: I say, I say, my groin
jokes.explanation: Good character voice mimicking cartoon rooster

jokes.text: I don't wanna fight no Union
jokes.punchline: Thinks he's still fighting Civil War
jokes.explanation: Good historical confusion humor with Southern character

jokes.text: I've become enchanted and illusioned with Washington
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism showing Krusty's confused state

jokes.text: That's right, over the homes of poor people
jokes.setup: Celebrating planes flying where they belong
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're now over poor neighborhoods
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark ending revealing the real 'solution' to the problem

tmdb_overview: An air traffic route is redirected so that airplanes start flying directly over the Simpsons' house. The noise starts to disrupt their lives and, desperate for a solution, the family turns to their Congressman. However, he dies of a heart attack, so they ask Krusty the Clown to run for Congress so that he can help them. Krusty is successful in his campaign, but finds that his air traffic bill is ignored by the veteran Congressmen. He soon becomes disillusioned with the system, so the Simpsons, with guidance from Walter Mondale, help him out by blackmailing one of his opponents and attaching the bill to a more popular one. The bill passes, and the air traffic is re-routed.
